leads.name: Belt Publishing
leads.title: Founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Cleveland press founded 2013 by Anne Trubek, publishing sophisticated nonfiction and fiction about the Rust Belt and Great Lakes region. Roughly 15 titles per year, well under the 25-title threshold. Shares Cushing-Malloy's Great Lakes geography (under two hours away), which makes local press checks and low-cost regional freight a concrete advantage. Named publisher email confirmed and MX-validated.

leads.name: Driftwood Press
leads.title: Managing Editor and Co-founder
leads.notes: Independent literary press and magazine founded 2013 (Tampa, relocated to Austin, TX in 2024), publishing poetry, fiction, and graphic novels plus an annual anthology; roughly six books a year; co-founded by managing editor James McNulty. Email RCPT-verified as a deliverable, existing mailbox (Google Workspace, non-catch-all). New to outreach, not a prior lead, not on the client list.

leads.name: JackLeg Press
leads.title: Editorial Team
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press (EIN on file) publishing six to ten full-length poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction collections a year, with a publishing schedule booked through 2028. US-authors-only and a no-generative-AI policy. No single named editorial contact is published, so the general editorial inbox is used and flagged here; greeting addresses the team. Email MX-validated. Headquartered in Washington, DC.

leads.name: Archipelago Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Nonprofit Brooklyn press devoted to translations of classic and contemporary world literature, founded by Jill Schoolman, publishing about 16 titles a year plus the Elsewhere Editions children's imprint. Internationally recognized, design-forward editions. Independent and active (Fall 2026/Spring 2027 catalog live). Uses general inbox (info@) which is flagged; named publisher identified. MX-validated.

leads.name: Wolfson Press
leads.title: Director
leads.notes: Second university source for this run: a publishing arm of Indiana University South Bend, directed by Kelcey Ervick, publishing poetry, fiction (American Storytellers series), memoir, and a civil-rights series. Low annual volume (well under 25). Strong Great Lakes proximity (about two hours from Ann Arbor). Director's institutional email (iu.edu) confirmed and MX-validated; the store domain is e-commerce only, so the IU address is used.

leads.name: Creston Books
leads.title: Editor-in-Chief and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Berkeley children's picture-book press founded by author-illustrator Marissa Moss, publishing about eight titles a year, many from debut creators, distributed by Lerner. Award-winning list. Children's category adds run diversity. Named publisher email on crestonbooks.co confirmed and MX-validated (Google MX).

leads.name: Parlor Press
leads.title: Founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent publisher (David Blakesley, founder) of scholarly and trade books, with the Free Verse Editions poetry series (three to four poetry titles a year) and the New Measure Poetry Prize. Named publisher email confirmed and MX-validated. Located in Anderson, SC (relocated from West Lafayette, IN).

leads.name: Holy Cow! Press
leads.title: Founding Editor and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Duluth press founded 1977 by Jim Perlman, committed to Midwest writers across poetry, fiction, and memoir, publishing roughly three to five titles a year (135-plus to date). Active 2025 list confirmed. Named publisher email confirmed and MX-validated (Google).

leads.name: Belle Point Press
leads.title: Founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Fort Smith, AR press founded 2021 by Casie Dodd (with Michael Dodd), celebrating Mid-South literary culture across poetry and prose, roughly four to six titles a year. Active and growing (regular Belle Point Bulletin). Role-based editor@ address tied to named founder; confirmed and MX-validated (Zoho).

leads.name: Bellevue Literary Press
leads.title: Publisher and Editorial Director
leads.notes: Independent nonprofit (since 2018) publishing literary fiction and nonfiction at the arts-and-sciences intersection, led by Erika Goldman, roughly six to eight award-recognized titles a year. Named publisher email and direct phone confirmed; MX-validated (Google).

leads.name: Unbound Edition Press
leads.title: Publisher and Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Independent Atlanta literary press, publisher and editor-in-chief Patrick Davis (executive editor Peter Campion), publishing a few thoughtfully selected poetry, fiction, memoir, and essay titles a year plus the journal Revel; distributed by Itasca Books. Named publisher email RCPT-verified as a deliverable, existing mailbox (Google Workspace, non-catch-all). New, not a prior lead, not on the client list.

leads.name: Grayson Books
leads.title: Owner and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent West Hartford, CT poetry press, owner-publisher Ginny Lowe Connors, publishing poetry collections, chapbooks, and themed anthologies (roughly four to six a year). Named publisher email confirmed and MX-validated (Google).

leads.name: Bauhan Publishing
leads.title: Owner and Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Peterborough, NH press (owner-publisher Sarah Bauhan) focused on New England regional history, art, nature, and poetry, with the May Sarton NH Poetry Prize; roughly four to six titles a year. Role-based news@ address tied to named owner, plus direct phone; MX-validated.

leads.name: Two Sylvias Press
leads.title: Co-founder and Editor
leads.notes: Independent Kingston, WA poetry press, co-founded by Kelli Russell Agodon and Annette Spaulding-Convy, publishing roughly three to four titles a year including the Wilder Series Prize. Active (2025 prize, regular newsletter). Shared press gmail used and flagged; named co-founder identified. MX-validated.

leads.name: Gunpowder Press
leads.title: Founding Editor
leads.notes: Independent Santa Barbara poetry nonprofit (501c3 since 2024), founded by David Starkey with co-editor Chryss Yost (both former Santa Barbara Poets Laureate), publishing four to six titles a year including the Shoreline Voices anthology series. Shared press gmail used and flagged; named editor identified. MX-validated.

leads.name: Sixteen Rivers Press
leads.title: Poetry Collective
leads.notes: Independent San Francisco shared-work nonprofit poetry collective publishing roughly two to three titles a year; spotlighted by UC Berkeley Library in 2026 (active). As a collective there is no single named editor, so the general inbox and phone are used and flagged; greeting addresses the collective. MX-validated (Google).

leads.name: Mayapple Press
leads.title: Founder, Publisher and Editor
leads.notes: Independent literary press founded 1978 by Judith Kerman (publisher and editor), publishing four to five titles a year across poetry, fiction, and Great Lakes/Northeast nonfiction, women's writing, and translation. Named publisher email confirmed and MX-validated. Based in Woodstock, NY.

leads.name: EastOver Press
leads.title: Co-founder and Editor
leads.notes: Independent literary press (est. 2020) publishing short story, essay, and poetry collections plus the online journal Cutleaf; editors include Denton Loving and Walter Robinson. Roughly six to eight titles a year. General inbox (info@) used and flagged; named co-founder/editor identified for the greeting. Listed address Rochester, MA. MX-validated.

leads.name: Transit Books
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Independent Bay Area nonprofit publishing six to eight award-winning literary translation and American titles a year, plus Transit Children's Editions; publishers Adam Z. Levy and Ashley Nelson Levy. Nobel and International Booker recognition. Publicity inbox used and flagged (named publishers identified). MX-validated (Google).

leads.name: Flashlight Press
leads.title: Editor and Art Director
leads.notes: Independent Brooklyn children's picture-book publisher (founded 2004), team includes editor and art director Shari Dash Greenspan; roughly three to four highly awarded titles a year (33 state award lists, 19 translations). Children's category adds run diversity. Role-based editor@ address tied to named editor; MX-validated.
